title: Xi asks Trump if U.S. and China can avoid 'Thucydides Trap' at high-stakes summit
description: U.S. President Donald Trump met Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday morning for the start of a high-stakes summit that runs through Friday.

title: In photos: Trump kicks off high-stakes summit with Xi
description: The high-stakes summit is expected to cover a wide range of contentious topics, including trade, technology, export controls, Taiwan, and the Iran war.

title: Oil struggles for direction as IEA flags greater volatility ahead, OPEC cuts demand forecast
description: OPEC cut its demand growth estimates for 2026 to about 1.2 million barrels per day.

title: Inside India newsletter: Trump-Xi meeting could test India’s positioning as China counterweight
description: For more than two decades, consecutive U.S. administrations have seen India as a counterbalance to China's growing influence in the Indo-Pacific.

title: Cerebras prices IPO above expected range, as Wall Street braces for AI tsunami
description: Cerebras raised $5.55 billion in its IPO, and with the chipmaker's offering, investors are gearing up for some even bigger AI deals later this year.

title: Asia-Pacific stocks trade mixed as Trump lands in Beijing for high-stakes Xi meeting
description: Asia markets traded mixed ahead of closely watched Trump-Xi summit on trade, tariffs and technology restrictions.

title: Cisco to cut jobs so it can invest more in AI, and the stock rockets toward a record
description: Cisco’s stock soars into record territory, after the networking giant announced plans to invest more in AI and upbeat earnings report.

title: Private equity cools on India amid lofty prices and economic jitters
description: Weaker dealmaking environment during turbulent period for country’s financial markets

title: Is Polaroid Coming for Today's Stock Market?
description: Market concentration in the AI Boom is even worse than in the Nifty Fifty era.

title: Japan’s 30-Year Bond Sale Demand Stronger Than 12-Month Average
description: Japan’s 30-year government bond auction Thursday saw slightly stronger demand than the 12-month average as higher yields underpinned buying.

title: Copper Retreats From Record Close as Purchases in China Slow
description: Copper retreated from a record-high close as the rally started to deter purchases in China, where investors were monitoring the summit between Chinese leader Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump.

title: Xi Says Common Interests Outweigh Difficulties
description: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping made their opening remarks at the Great Hall of the People, with Xi saying the common interests between the United States and China outweigh their differences. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: What India’s Top Three Mutual Funds Bought and Sold in April
description: Indian investors plowed money into equity funds for a 62nd straight month in April, though inflows eased from the previous month.

title: Iran War Gives Xi The Chance to Rekindle Gas Sales With Trump
description: As the leaders of the world’s two largest economies meet in Beijing, energy markets will be watching closely for any breakthrough in a dispute that has reshaped global fuel flows: China’s tariffs on US oil and gas.

title: Sarah Beran on US-China Summit
description: Sarah Beran, Former US Embassy Deputy Chief of Mission in Beijing and Partner at Macro Advisory Partners, says both Washington and Beijing have significantly lowered their expectations for the summit. She shares her outlook on the highly anticipated meeting between the world’s two largest economies. She speaks with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud Watts on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Xi Jinping Greets Trump With Lavish Ceremony
description: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping met at a welcome ceremony featuring honor guards and carefully choreographed pageantry. Bloomberg's Annmarie Hordern reports from Beijing. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Metal Stocks Stand Out in India’s Struggling Market
description: Nifty Metal Index has overtaken the FMCG gauge in market value.

title: China's Xi Greets Trump in Beijing
description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping welcomed US President Donald Trump at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on Thursday. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Qantas Boss Vanessa Hudson Turns Screw on Ailing Air New Zealand
description: Qantas Airways Ltd. Chief Executive Officer Vanessa Hudson is ramping up pressure on struggling Air New Zealand Ltd., adding close to 1 million seats on trans-Tasman routes to squeeze the ailing rival.

title: Japanese Crude Tanker Emerges Outside Hormuz in Rare Transit
description: A Japanese supertanker has emerged in the Gulf of Oman after last signaling that it was inside the Persian Gulf, indicating a rare, undercover transit through the Strait of Hormuz by a vessel from the Asian country.

title: Bagmane REIT Set for Debut in Test for India’s IPO Market
description: Blackstone Inc.-backed Bagmane Prime Office REIT is set to start trading in Mumbai on Thursday after raising $360 million in an initial public offering, a debut that will offer cues on investor appetite and the outlook for India’s equity capital markets.

title: Hunt Oil Boss Sees Middle East Conflict as ‘Nightmare Scenario’
description: Hunter Hunt, the grandson of famed Texas oil baron H.L. Hunt, is concerned about the impact from damaged Middle Eastern energy infrastructure that could leave oil production lower for years to come.

title: War Turmoil Saps Asian Reserves With Philippines, India Hit Most
description: Foreign-exchange reserves are slumping across Asia as policymakers shell out funds to defend their currencies against the spike in oil prices caused by the Iran war.

title: What's At Stake as Xi and Trump Meet in Beijing
description: President Donald Trump is in Beijing for the first state visit to China by a US leader in nine years, as the world’s two largest economies look to stabilize ties against the backdrop of the Iran war. Bloomberg's Stephen Engle breaks down what is at stake. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Chip IPO Highlights Malaysia Deals Surging Toward 13-Year High
description: Initial public offerings are booming in Malaysia and a wave of upcoming listings including by a chip design firm is powering the market toward the biggest volume in 13 years.

title: A Watchful Beijing Tightens Security for Trump’s Visit
description: Historic sites and roads have been closed in the Chinese capital for President Trump’s state visit, resulting in severe traffic and disappointed tourists.

title: Judge Orders U.S. to Return Colombian Woman Deported to Congo
description: The unusual ruling came after the judge found that the Trump administration had most likely violated the law by deporting the 55-year-old woman to the African country despite its refusal to take her.

title: Why Does China Want Taiwan?
description: There are few issues in diplomacy more complicated than the status of the self-governing island, which China claims as its own. It is almost certain to come up when President Trump meets China’s leader.

title: Activists, Lawmakers Urge Trump to Call for Release of Jimmy Lai and Dong Yuyu
description: Supporters of Mr. Lai, a Hong Kong dissident, and Mr. Dong, a Chinese journalist, hope President Trump will raise their cases with President Xi Jinping.

title: Here’s the latest.
The China Gambit: From Nixon to Trump
description: President Trump’s visit to Beijing comes as relations with China have become more combative.

title: As Trump and Xi Meet in China, American and Chinese People Are Drifting Apart
description: Past meetings between the presidents of the U.S. and China have involved friendly displays of cultural exchange. Those gestures have largely dried up.

title: Alberta Separation Petition Is Dealt a Court Blow
description: A judge sided with an Indigenous group that had asked for a petition by separatists to force a vote on the oil-rich province seceding from Canada to be thrown out.

title: Mamdani Urges State to Block Western Union’s Deal for Intermex
description: New York City’s mayor said the company’s proposed acquisition of Intermex could make it costlier for immigrants to send money abroad.

title: US Army recovers remains of second soldier reported missing during Moroccan exercise
description: The Army identified the remains of Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, one of the two soldiers reported missing on May 2.

title: Alibaba shares surge 7% in Hong Kong as firm accelerates pivot to AI
description: Shares of Alibaba Group Holding opened up 7.8 per cent in Hong Kong, after the firm reported strong growth from its artificial intelligence products and ramped up its commitment to the technology. The Chinese tech giant also saw its New York-listed shares surge 8.2 per cent on Wednesday, closing at US$145.81, after it confirmed that revenues from AI-related products had hit 8.97 billion yuan (US$1.3 billion) in the first quarter. The company said the segment had now registered triple-digit...

title: The Pentagon Has Less Than 8 Months to Break China’s Rare Earth Grip
description: China moved this week to take tighter control of its rare earths producers amid an escalating tit-for-tat battle with Washington over everything from tariffs and semiconductor warfare to pressure over Iran and Hormuz. At the same time, an ominous Pentagon deadline looms large, with the entire American defense system to be banned from using any Chinese-origin rare earths materials beginning in January 2027. With fewer than eight months on the clock, Washington is sounding the alarms at full blast, and the Department of War is leading the clarion…
